entry.title: Re: Digital Skills Initiative Applicant Guide (For self-study candidates)
entry.content:
I’m Arsala, a dedicated SAP SuccessFactors consultant with two years of experience in delivering cutting-edge HR solutions. My expertise in leveraging SuccessFactors modules has enabled me to enhance organizational efficiency and drive strategic HR initiatives effectively. With a solid foundation in configuring and implementing various SuccessFactors components, including PMGM, EC Payroll, Time Management and EC, I bring both technical proficiency and a passion for innovation to every project.

My experience has honed my ability to address complex challenges and deliver impactful results. I thrive in collaborative environments and am committed to continuous learning to stay ahead of industry trends. Joining the SAP Digital Initiatives Program would be the perfect opportunity for me to combine my expertise with cutting-edge technology to revolutionize HR processes in various industries. I look forward to contributing to transformative HR projects and supporting organizational growth through tailored SuccessFactors solutions.

it is hard to judge that from a distance. It would be great if you could open a case so that SAP support could take a look into your system. Most probably componen BC-OP-LNX would be the correct one

thanks and kind regards
Karin

entry.title: Re: can be closed
entry.content:
Hello @urb 

Check if the note 1492431 - stock change before count. Inv not cleared or wrong quantity helps. Otherwise report the issue to SAP support.

Best regards

Dominik Tylczynski

entry.title: Re: Create Modify or delete role objects using BApi
entry.content:
I tried to find PRGN_1252_AUTH_ADD or something related functions PRGN_1252_AUTH_CHANGE and not exist. And BAPI_ROLE_ACTGROUP_ASSIGN is just for user assign role, and BAPI_ROLE_SAVE not exist, and i want to use Bapi remotely.. If there is any existing in sap or what bapi do I need to develop to get it.

Regards

entry.title: Re: Upload Excel using SAP RAP Only
entry.content: Very useful blog. Thanks for sharing.

entry.title: What we think about the second meetup in New York
entry.content:
A Look Back at Our Second NYC #SAP Meetup

We just had our second #SAP meetup in New York City, and it was fantastic! It was a smaller group this time, but everyone was really into SAP and excited to be there.

We planned to start the event two hours before sunset, which allowed us to enjoy the park's light and gave participants enough time to arrive. The park was an excellent choice for our meetup. Its quiet and comfortable atmosphere was perfect for us to get to know each other better and have meaningful conversations.

 

Meet Some of Our Attendees

One of the attendees, Richard, was especially excited. He’s currently an accountant in the fashion industry but is passionate about technology. He’s been learning about SAP FICO on his own after hearing about SAP's strength in big companies. He came to the meetup eager to meet others and grow his network.

Another attendee has recently moved to the U.S. and is a director at his company. Like many of us, he wasn’t born in the U.S., which added to our diverse mix. We ended up sharing stories about visas and the immigration process, which got quite a few laughs!

Despite our initial timing, we decided to stay longer because we had fluid conversations and a lot to talk about. We were a diverse group of developers, functional experts, data scientists, and Basis team members.

What’s Next?

We’re already planning a big event, probably a SIT, for this winter or next year and a relaxed get-together next month. Stay tuned for more details, and we hope to see you there! 
The goal is to find new attendees who are ready to share and be an event speakers.

In the end, thanks to everyone who came. The park's great location, your energy, and stories made this meetup special. Let's keep the spirit alive as we continue to learn and connect through SAP!

entry.title: What's New in SAP Asset Performance Management 2409
entry.content:
In our latest release, we’ve made significant improvements to help you work smarter and faster, for example, we've simplified and enhanced the user experience in both risk and criticality assessments and RCM assessments. We've also introduced some great new features in recommendation management improving traceability with comments for status changes. Whether you're in the field or the office, these updates increase efficiency and usability across the board. Read on to learn more about all the new features!

 

Asset Risk Assessment

Enhanced UX for Risk & Criticality Assessments: Simplified Scale Definitions

We’re excited to unveil significant UX improvements for the the risk and criticality assessment. Previously, accessing essential scale definitions required multiple clicks, which was particularly cumbersome for users on tablets. To enhance the experience, we’ve introduced a new table view that seamlessly displays long descriptions directly within the assessment screen. This update eliminates the need for additional clicks and ensures a smoother, more efficient workflow, especially for those using tablets in the field.

This update not only streamlines the process but also integrates seamlessly with existing features, including the Financial Risk field, Notes section, and the 10x10 matrix. Looking ahead, it also accommodates the future requirement for Leading Failure Modes.

With these enhancements, Reliability Engineers can now view all relevant information in one place, improving efficiency and ease of use. This change reflects our commitment to refining user experience and responding to feedback, ensuring a more intuitive and effective assessment process.

Watch the video:

Asset Reliability Engineering

Select Multiple Recommendations for Rework and View Comments with Status Changes

You can now select multiple recommendations and simultaneously create a comment for all, enhancing both efficiency and the ability to track the history of the recommendation. Rework can be done for recommendations with status:

  • Released

  • Implementation in Process

With this, the recommendation will be set to status In Process and sent back to the Manage Recommendations application for reliability engineer to reprocess the recommendation. You can view the most recent comments on the list page using the Comment icon. 

Watch the video:

For more information, see the SAP Help Portal Implement Recommendations.

Enhanced Recommendation Management: Commenting on Status Changes

Recording comments during status changes provides context and traceability in recommendation management. The Information tab has a Comments section to view the recorded comments.

You can record comments for recommendation with the following statuses:

  • In Process → On Hold

  • In Process → Rejected

  • On Hold → In Process

  • For Review → In process

  • For Review → On Hold

  • For Review → Rejected

  • Ready for Release → In Process

  • Ready for Release → On Hold

  • Ready for Release → Released

You can also view the most recent comments by choosing the Comment icon on the list view page. 

Watch the video to see it in action:

Support Multiple Technical Objects in RCM Assessments

You can now select and assess multiple technical objects simultaneously as long as they share the same class, failure data profile, and planning plant details. For more information, see Assigning Technical Objects.

Watch the video: 

Improved UX in RCM Assessment - New Three Panel Layout

A three panel layout is used to display the failure data and associated evaluations. The Create or Edit Consequence Assessment buttons allow you to view the consequence evaluation using the third panel. On the Assessment page, there is an option to enter full screen mode. Watch the video to find out more:

APIS for FMEA Assessments

You can use public APIS to create, update, read, and delete FMEA assessments. For more information, see API Services.

 

Keep In Touch

Here's a quick reminder how to keep in touch with all things APM:

  • Check out all past and present Intelligent Asset Management webcasts here. If you register for an IAM webcast, you will be automatically subscribed to our IAM Community Engagement Newsletter. 
  • Check some of our recent blogs:
    • Why Failure-Mode Focus Matters in Intelligent Asset Management 
    • What's New SAP Asset Performance Management 2408
    • What's New SAP Asset Performance Management 2407 
    • What's New SAP Asset Performance Management 2406 
    • What's New SAP Asset Performance Management 2405 
    • What's New SAP Asset Performance Management 2404
    • SAP Asset Performance Management Embedding Cumulocity IoT to Drive Innovations in IoT and AI
    • How AI-based Anomaly Detection empowers reliability engineers in SAP Asset Performance Management
    • SAP Asset Performance Management – FMEA (Failure Mode and Effects Analysis)
    • What's New SAP Asset Performance Management 2403
  • Subscribe to What's New on the SAP Help Portal
  • Submit or vote for the improvement you would like to see in the SAP Continuous Influence session

See you again soon for the 2410 release update!

entry.title: Localization of simulations in SAP Companion
entry.content:
Hi,

I have a question regarding  the localization of simulations in SAP Companion.

I have both recorded simulations in English and in French relating to the same processes, and also set the appropriate language flags relating to the simulation’s languages.

In the Learning section of SAP Companion, when my FIORI interface is in English, I only see the English simulations, the French content is hidden, so in this case everything works fine.

Nevertheless, when I switch the UI to French, I see both English and French simulations, so the English content is not hidden in this case.

Is there something I could have forget to set?

entry.title: Re: I want to have an app like CNMASSCREATE in S4 HANA Pr...
entry.content:
Most of the standard PS transactions have not been replaced by Fiori Apps, they will still work in Fiori.  Use the Fiori Apps Library to see what's new.

https://fioriappslibrary.hana.ondemand.com/sap/fix/externalViewer/

There isn't a direct replacement for CNMASSCREATE . Go into Fiori, App Finder and select the SAP Menu tab and search for your transaction. It will find it.

entry.title: Re: How PRod / Preview datacenter can have a feature down...
entry.content:
Not the best move by SAP. I had a UAT testing session yesterday with a customer and we couldn't do much testing with onChange rule freezing everything.

As for the question on where to find info on patch for people who read the thread, I go here: https://community.sap.com/t5/patches/tkb-p/hxm-patches

I agree that there isn't enough info (and nothing on onChange rule and the lastest patch!) but it is a start to explain why my instances aren't behaving like usual!

Cheers,

Stéphanie

entry.title: Re: Link Service Job after MIGO
entry.content:
Hello @Cynthia_A 

You can't modify accounting details of a material document once it is posted. Even if you modify account assignment of the purchase order item, it will impact only material document posted afterwards, not the ones already posted.

You would need to cancel the material document, update the PO accounting assignment and post the material document again.

Best regards

Dominik Tylczynski

entry.title: A Practical Guide to Naming iFlows in SAP Cloud Integration (SCI)
entry.content:
In SAP Cloud Integration (SCI), naming iflows clearly and consistently is crucial for maintainability and support. A structured naming convention helps teams quickly understand an iflow’s purpose, its integration partners, and its data flow. At CLAIMATE Tech, we follow a well-defined approach to naming our iflows, and here’s our guide to making it work for you.

Our Naming Template

We use the following format for naming iflows in SCI:

SCI<iflowid>_<direction>_<iflowdesc>

Each part of this structure serves a specific purpose. Let’s break it down:

 

SCI<iflowid>

The unique identifier of the iflow.

  • <class> A 3-digit code representing the message class (e.g., 000 for utility messages or 002 for partner integrations).
  • <id> A 5-digit identifier, structured as XXXYY.
  • XXX Our internal partner ID for easy identification (e.g., 001 for LumixTech, 002 for VeloCorp).
  • YY Sequence number for the message, usually incremented in steps of 10 or 5 (e.g., 10 for order submission, 20 for acknowledgment).
 

<direction>

The data flow direction.

  • IN: Inbound
  • OUT: Outbound
  • INT: Internal (non-partner-related utility iflow)
 

<iflowdesc>

A flexible description of the iflow, typically used for partner integrations.

It usually follows the structure:

  • <partner>_<senderAdapter>_<receiverAdapter>_<msgName>

where

  • <partner> A human-readable name for the partner (e.g., LumixTech, VeloCorp).
  • <senderAdapter> and <receiverAdapter> The adapters used (e.g., HTTP, SFTP, ProcessDirect).
  • <msgName> The message or process name (e.g., OrderRequest, InvoiceSubmission).

Example iFlows

Here are two examples of well-named iflows:

SCI00200150_IN_VeloCorp_HTTP_SFTP_OrderRequest

Explanation: This iflow handles an inbound (IN) integration for partner VeloCorp, using HTTP as the sender adapter and SFTP as the receiver adapter, with the message type OrderRequest. Message is from class 002 which can be eg. Partner integration and is 001 is lookup id for partner VeloCorp. This message has its id 50 so orderRequests from VeloCorp and its utility iflow starts with digit 5.

 

SCI00200151_INT_VeloCorp_HTTP_SFTP_OrderRequest_PostProcess

Explanation: This is an internal iflow that serves as a first (51)helper for the OrderRequests (51) integration with VeloCorp, handling PostProcess logic for the OrderRequest message, also using HTTP and SFTP adapters.

 

Why Naming Conventions Matter

Having a consistent naming convention allows for:

  • Easier troubleshooting: Issues can be quickly traced to specific iflows.
  • Improved supportability: The team can easily understand each iflow without needing extensive documentation.
  • Enhanced collaboration: Clear naming rules allow different teams to work on the same integration projects more efficiently.

Standardizing Naming for New Projects

Each project or purpose or team should have its own class number. That class number must be unique. Also ID for partners must be unique for class and ID of message must be unique per partner.

Maintaining a shared list of these IDs and their purposes in a central knowledge base—like Excel, Notion, or Azure DevOps—would ensure clarity and prevent duplication.

 

Conclusion

Following these naming guidelines will make your iflows easier to manage, support, and collaborate on within SAP Cloud Integration. Remember: a little consistency goes a long way!

entry.title: Re: Demystifying Document Management Service on BTP
entry.content:
I think the information suggesting that storing archive files generated via SARA is probably misleading. We could not get this working and eventually raised a ticket with SAP, only to receive a response that ADK integration to BTP DMS (CMIS) is not part of the feature list:

BTP DMS Service can not be used as Storage Solution for Archive files. CMIS is not supported by ADK (Archive development Kit).
It is not part of the feature list of BTP DMS Service

Now, if I go to BTP I would like to try to generate simple Fiory app using generator.

I entered there an full URL for the service, now I do not know what to enter as Service user name and Service password. I tried to use my account credentional which I use when I login into BTP Trial account but it doesn't work. Where I take these values from please?

entry.title: Re: ABAP MailOutput Content-Type:text/html; charset="us-a...
entry.content: If the receiver of the mail can't handle quoted-printable (=3D=22 etc.) then you could perhaps try Content-Transfer-Encoding: 8bit

entry.title: Re: How to use cl_gui_alv_tree->add_node correctly?
entry.content:
A note to <<Obsolete: "call method obj_tree->add_node" (instead, use "obj_tree->add_node").>>:

Pretty printer in windows gui works good with method calls like "call method obj_tree->add_node". It does not work with calls like "obj_tree->add_node( ... )".

Alex

entry.title: Re: SAP EWM Stuck Queue - QM processing currently only po...
entry.content: Hello Jack, I am having embedded EWM in S4 HANA 2023 core

entry.title: How to transport a Data Model conversion to New Model Type
entry.content:
Dear SAP Analytics Cloud community,

Currently we are looking into the conversion of Classic Account Models to the New Model Type and are curious to read over some experiences and see whether there are any solutions for our challenges.

We have been using SAP Analytics Cloud from the very beginning and Data Models were initially created as the Classic Account Model. With regard to the future and the desire to use new capabilities of the New Model Type we would like to convert our models. Our landscape consists out of separate development, acceptance and production systems. Ideally, we would like to prepare the conversion in our development system as a change and transport it throughout the landscape.

Is there any documentation on best practices for the conversion of Data Models?

Using our transport system would mean that we lose production data in the migrated model after importing the change. Performing the conversion in the production system would mean more risks and downtime of reports and other background processes such as loading and transforming data with Multi/Data Actions.

We are curious about your experience.

entry.title: Accrual reversal for customer Rebate with different customer in the accruals
entry.content:
Hi,

We have a scenario where in we need to create a condition contract with rebate receiver customer1 but when we do the business volume selection, we use the invoices/order from customer2, customer 3, customer 4.

Now, if we need to settle it, things are working okay. But if we want to do the accrual reversal- the system post back the accrued value to customer1 instead of customer2/3/4.

See example below of how we set up the condition contract. 

How do we handle this?

entry.title: Re: STO Between Storage locations
entry.content: @VKR_23 Thanks for the detailed info. Is it possible to use the stock type of storage location in the STO process? For example, I have two storage locations naming 0100 and 0200 where I want to transfer the stock from 0100 (Blocked Stock) to 0200 (Unrestricted Use)? Thanks in advance for your help.

Regards,
HH

entry.title: Two Tier ERP Transactional Data Replication options
entry.content:
Two Tier ERP Integration:

Integration is crucial for Two-Tier ERP scenarios, as understanding end-to-end business processes, including both master and transactional data flows, is vital for customer success. It’s important to identify integration requirements early in the process. SAP S/4HANA Cloud will also need to integrate with various other solutions within the customer’s system landscape. Two-Tier ERP offers multiple deployment options, For instance, let's look into one of the deployement option where companies running their main ERP systems (headquarters) on SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud Edition or SAP S/4HANA on-premise can connect to subsidiaries using SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud Edition.

Two Tier ERP Transactional Data Integration options

For easier understanding, we have separated integration into two categories: Master Data and Transactional Data. This blog focuses on the options for transactional data integration, while you can find the master data options in this linked blog https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blogs-by-sap/two-tier-erp-master-data-replication-options/ba-p/13879970

Option 1: BTP Integration Suite - Cloud Integration

Top 5 reasons to choose SAP Integration Suite

  1. Tackle ANY integration challenge with a holistic approach.
  2. Accelerate business outcomes with out-of-the-box integration.
  3. Innovate faster with an open API platform for the future.
  4. Speed time to value.
  5. Outperform with enterprise-grade integration.

The Integration Suite is applicable in the following Two-Tier use cases:

  • Headquarters is using S/4HANA on-premise, S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition, or S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, while subsidiaries are on S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.
  • The customer has a mixed landscape, including non-SAP solutions that need to integrate with headquarters.
  • The customer is currently using SAP S/4HANA Cloud editions and plans to expand to additional applications in the future.
  • The customer already has a Business Technology Platform (BTP) account with the Integration Suite.

Key Aspects of SAP Integration Suite from a Two-Tier ERP Integration Perspective:

  • SAP Cloud Integration enables seamless connections between both cloud and on-premise systems, whether they are SAP or non-SAP.
  • It supports real-time scenarios and provides a wide range of pre-built integration content (Integration Packages), along with extensive capabilities and connectivity options.
  • Integration Flows (iFlows) allow you to design message processing logic and configure sender/receiver adapters within SAP Cloud Integration.

You can find details of one of the Two-Tier ERP Buyer and Supplier scenario integration flow here https://api.sap.com/package/IntegrationofSAPS4HANACloudProcurementwithOnPremiseSuppliersS4HANAorERP/integrationflow

Option 2: SAP Business Accelerator Hub

Don’t have an SAP BTP account with the Integration Suite service? No worries—you can still meet your integration needs through the API Hub.

  • Seamlessly discover content that addresses all your integration and extension requirements.
  • Explore integrations between SAP products as well as between SAP and third-party applications.
  • Browse various products, categories, business processes, partners, featured APIs, integrations, and more here https://api.sap.com/search?searchterm=Two%20Tier&tab=All&$refinedBy=true

The key takeaway of this blog is our recommendation to use the SAP Integration Suite service for transactional data replication wherever applicable, allowing you to leverage pre-built integration content for your scenarios.

Future Exploration
In upcoming blogs, we will explore Master Data and Transactional Data in greater detail, and discuss how they can benefit your business.

entry.title: Re: Analysis for SAC - Scheduling possible?
entry.content:
Hi @mario_panzenboeck,
 
even if the topic is a few years old, I want to inform you that as of today it is perfectly possible to plan and customize schedules for both AfO workbooks saved on SAC and for the SAC Stories and Applications themselves.
 
This is possible through “APOS Publisher for Cloud” which is a program developed by APOS Systems (an SAP partner).
You can schedule, program and customize publishing from SAC as you prefer, it practically has the same publishing capabilities and dynamisms as BO and even more.
 
If you want to take a look this is our site:
https://www.apos.com/
 
These, instead, are the direct links to the Publisher for Cloud solution and to the specific segment for AfO:
https://www.apos.com/content/apos-publisher-sap-analytics-cloud
https://www.apos.com/content/apos-publisher-cloud-sap-analysis-for-office
 
 
 
Hope this helps!
Best regards,
Tommaso

entry.title: Restrict duplicate invoices only for a certain company code
entry.content:
Dear Team,

 

We have a requirement to restrict the posting of Vendor duplicate invoices at MIRO level for specific company code.

Currently we have 5 company codes but we want to restrict duplicate invoices(Error message) only for one specific company code. 

I have maintained the below configurational settings

1.OMRDC- Selct Check boxes for Company code, Reference number and Invoice date

2.OMRM/OBA5- Maintained message class “108 & 462” to convert the warning message to Error message

3.XK02- Select duplicate invoice check box in Vendor master data level

Even after maintaining all the above configurations, system is able to restrict the duplicate invoice for the requested company code but this also affects all other remaining company codes.

The combination for restricting the duplicate invoice—Same Company code, Same vendor code, same invoice date and same reference number. Should exclude this restriction for reversal invoice document.

Please suggest us the alternative solution to restrict the posting of duplicate invoice at MIRO level only for Specific company code(DIGITS) without being affecting for all other remaining company codes.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Restrict duplicate invoices only for a certain compan...
entry.content: This requirement is currently not available in standard. Duplicate invoice check will work the same for all company codes.

entry.title: Re: SAP Yard Management and SAP Yard Logistics Deployment Options – embedded vs. side-by-side
entry.content:
Can you describe or provide an OSS explaining the " SAP Yard Logistics 2.0 on SCM server includes functional limitations" stated above?

Also... is it possible to install YL200 on EWM 9.5 client and not use PI in between?

entry.title: Purchase Order Split based on Plant ( Site )
entry.content:
Hi Team,

I have a requirement, User need : While PO creation ,if the PO has two line items one with Plant A and another with Plant B , then the PO has to be split into two new POs based on the new different plants. 

Actually the user is using the standard Fiori app " Manage Purchase Orders " to create new Pos.

I can't able to find any enhancement or Badi in S4 to achieve this requirement. I have logic in my hand but i don't know where it can be placed so that the split can be done successfully.  

Can experts help on this?

entry.title: SAP TM: Possibility to default a Product when adding Package to Freight Order
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

When adding item in terms of a Package (Pallet) for the Freight Order. Is it then possible to default a Product in this field. As it is a mandatory field, and the process always requires the same Material/Product for this field, it would be nice to have it already filled.

Br,

Benjamin

entry.title: Re: 🟡 Techtoberfest 2024
entry.content: GitHub Copilot - My favorite VSCode Extension 😁

entry.title: Transport Request assignment in SAP Cloud ALM Feature with "in Testing" Status
entry.content:
Hi,

We observed that while importing transports via SAP Cloud ALM that we are able to assign transports while a Feature is in status "In Testing".

Ideally, if we have to add any TR's to a Feature then we should have to move the status back to "in Implementation" first, then assign the TR. This would match the behaviour in SAP Solution Manager 7.2 ChaRM. But in SAP Cloud ALM we are able to add TR's in Features with status "In implementation" and "in Testing".

Could you please let us know if this is an inconsistency which will be figured out by SAP and corrected, or if SAP Cloud ALM is designed deliberately in such a way. Please clarify.

 

 

 

Regards,

 

Debasis


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Transport Request assignment in SAP Cloud ALM Feature...
entry.content:
The SAP Help Portal Application Help for SAP Cloud ALM documents this in the Status Flow for Features page.
It clarifies that the behaviour you encounter is by design.
Transport Requests can be assigned for Features in these statuses:

  • In Specification
  • In Implementation
  • In Testing

If you wish to influence the future development of SAP Cloud ALM and its features, please examine the SAP Customer Influence campaign related to SAP Cloud ALM for Implementation.

entry.title: Mastering the Terraform Provider for SAP BTP: FAQ Style
entry.content:
With more than 100,000 downloads in 2024, the Terraform Provider for SAP BTP has been making waves among our customers and partners. To assist with some of the most common questions, I’ve pulled together this FAQ guide, topping it off with resources that I hope you’ll find useful. 

But first, what exactly is Terraform? According to its developer, HashiCorp, "Terraform is an Infrastructure as Code tool that enables you to define cloud and on-prem resources in human-readable configuration files. These files can be versioned, reused, and shared, allowing for a consistent and streamlined workflow to manage your infrastructure." Check out this Intro to Terraform by HashiCorp to get a quick snapshot of what it is all about. 

What does Terraform Provider for BTP enable? 

  • Managing Global Accounts, Directories, and Subaccounts 
  • Assigning Entitlements & Subscriptions 
  • Managing Environments (Cloud Foundry, Kyma) 
  • Managing Service Instances and Service Bindings 
  • Setting up Trust Configuration 
  • Creating Roles and Role Collections 
  • Assign Role Collections 

Why use it? 

In short, it cuts down on manual errors, prevents over-provisioning, and makes resource scaling more efficient, all while standing out for its unique suitability to multi-cloud environments and its user-friendly template language. Plus, its integration with CI/CD pipelines and GitOps practices makes continuous monitoring and infrastructure optimization much easier. 

  • Automation: Streamlines SAP BTP resource management through automated scripts. 
  • Consistency: Ensures consistent configuration and deployment of resources. 
  • Simplified Multi-Cloud Infrastructure Management:  Terraform lets you use the same workflow to manage multiple providers and handle cross-cloud dependencies.  
  • Version Control: Manages infrastructure changes using version control systems. 
  • Scalability: Easily scales resources up or down based on demand. 
  • Disaster Recovery: Can facilitate backup and recovery processes for SAP BTP resources. 

Are there any costs associated with it? 

The Terraform Provider for SAP BTP is free of charge. Generally, no direct costs are associated with its use, but it’s recommended that users or companies verify this internally to ensure compliance with any licensing considerations. For further details on Terraform usage and licensing, refer to HashiCorp's License FAQ. 

How to get started? 

  • Try it out: Start by installing Terraform, creating your first simple subaccount, and exploring our Git repository with samples that provide hands-on guidance and try out this tutorial on developers.sap.com. 
  • See it in action: As part of our recent webcast series of SAP’s BTP DevOps and observability tools, we have conducted also sessions featuring Terraform provider for BTP with Lecture and Demo. 
  • Get more advanced: Explore this list of advanced scenarios by Christian Lechner that go beyond day 1 Terraform use cases.  
  • Engage with the SAP's Terraform communities and follow experts like Christian Lechner - (@lechnerc77 ), Rui Nogueira ( @Rui-Nogueira ) Stephan Cherian, Vipin VijayKumar, Boris Zarske.(@BorisZarske) and others on LinkedIn for the latest updates.  

How to incorporate Terraform in CI/CD Pipelines and DevOps workflows?  

You can easily do that using tools like  GitHub Actions, GitLab CI, Jenkins, Azure DevOps, or SAP’s Project Piper. These tools help automate Terraform commands like init, plan, validate, and apply, letting you set up workflows that automatically test, review, and apply infrastructure changes from a version-controlled repository.

Tools like ArgoCD or Flux can also take this process further by continuously watching Git repositories for changes and automatically applying them to keep your infrastructure in the desired state.

Are there ways to expand the capabilities of the Terraform Provider for BTP? 

Terraform providers, including the one for BTP, are delivered as binaries and do not offer extension points. Having said that the providers are published as open source. So, if you are missing features, you can open a feature request or if you would like to contribute yourself you can do so. The providers are written in Go, so with the fitting development skill feel free to contribute. 

Is it possible to retrieve the current state of an existing BTP Account? 

In general, Terraform offers the functionality of imports to bring existing resources under the management of Terraform. While this procedure works, it is cumbersome and error prone. Being aware of that we are developing an import functionality that will allow you to bring your existing BTP setup under Terraform's management in a streamlined and automated way. Stay tuned for more updates coming soon! 

How does the Terraform Provider for BTP compare to "alternative" tools? 

  • Terraform Provider vs. BTP CLI: The BTP CLI is ideal for direct, interactive management via imperative commands; Terraform is better suited for automated, scalable infrastructure management. 
  • Terraform Provider vs. SAP Automation Pilot:  Automation Pilot is better suited for operational task automation. 
  • Terraform vs/plus other tools:  For a clear overview of the essential tools and how to use them effectively., check out this informative blog post by Christian Lechner. 
  • “Earlier” alternatives: Before the launch of the TF provider for BTP, other tools were being used like the SAP BTP Boosters embedded into the cockpit or SAP BTP Setup Automator, however, none of them have the power and flexibility of Terraform. 

What are some key best practices? 

  • Use version Control: Store Terraform configurations in a version control system (e.g., Git) to track changes and maintain auditability. 
  • Manage state Remotely: Store state files in remote backends (e.g., S3, Terraform Cloud) with encryption and access controls; enable state locking to prevent concurrent changes. 
  • Modularize configurations: Organize code into reusable modules for consistency and version modules for controlled updates. 
  • Automate testing: Utilize tools like Terratest for automated testing and validation against security policies. 
  • Secure sensitive data: Handle sensitive data securely using environment variables or secret management tools. 

What are the challenges of using IaC/Terraform? 

  • Learning curve: HashiCorp Configuration Language (HCL) is easy to learn, but adopting IaC can be challenging especially for complex scenarios. 
  • Choosing the right toolset: Many tools are multipurpose; choosing the wrong one can cause unforeseen issues and complicate migration. 
  • Maintaining strong security: Requires good knowledge and active use of Terraform Security best practices, which is sometimes overlooked. 
  • Change of culture and organizational setup: Adoption of IaC especially on a large scale might require shift from a traditional DevOps team setup to a model that separates it into Application Lifecycle Management and Platform Team (Infrastructure and Security Lifecycles) approach.  

What could be the next steps in your Terraform journey? 

Use Developer Platforms: Platforms like Backstage offer a unified portal that enhances collaboration and visibility for IaC workflows. 

Supercharge Terraform with GenAI: While dedicated Terraform-specific GenAI tools are still emerging, you can leverage existing AI models like OpenAI's Codex or build custom integrations using APIs to enhance Terraform workflows. The possibilities range from automated code generation and intelligent infrastructure recommendations to AI-driven compliance and optimization strategies. Here are couple of esamples: 

  • OpenAI Codex or GitHub Copilot: Can be used to auto-generate Terraform code snippets or entire configurations based on natural language descriptions. It integrates directly into IDEs like Visual Studio Code, providing real-time suggestions and completions for Terraform code. 
  • Terraform Cloud or Terraform Enterprise: Facilitates the integration of AI tools into your Terraform workflows by automating the plan, apply, and state management processes.  
  • Terratest: Can be combined with GenAI tools to create dynamic test cases and scenarios for Terraform infrastructure, ensuring configurations are validated before deployment. 
  • Policy as Code Tools (e.g., Sentinel, Open Policy Agent): Used to automatically suggest or enforce policies based on best practices, security standards, and organizational requirements. 
  • Terraform Linting Tools (e.g., tflint, tfsec): GenAI tools can work with linting tools to provide enhanced analysis, such as suggesting optimizations or refactoring code based on learned patterns. 

How to level up Terraform skills? 

  • Watch Tutorials: Check out HashiCorp's YouTube channel. 
  • Join Events: Attend HashiConf, HashiDays, or local meetups in your area 
  • Stay Updated: Subscribe to the "weekly.tf" newsletter. 
  • Read Docs: Explore the official Terraform documentation.to 
  • Practice: Use HashiCorp Learn Labs. 
  • Engage: Join the  HashiCorp Discuss and Terraform Slack 

Any questions or feature requests? 

We’d love to hear from you! Feel free to share feedback, report issues, or suggest features on our GitHub repository.

Happy Terraforming! 

Disclaimer: This FAQ guide is here to help you get started with the SAP BTP Terraform Provider, but keep in mind it’s not official documentation. For the most accurate and up-to-date info, be sure to check out the official Terraform Registry documentation. 

P.S. Big thanks to my colleague Christian Lechner(@lechnerc77 ) for his valuable input on this FAQ guide and for always writing great blogs on this topic that I’ve been able to refer to.

entry.title: Re: where the smartlink in my inbox
entry.content:
Related Apps will appear once they have the same Sematic Object - in this case 'Material'.
Also make sure that the User does have the Authorization for the new created target mapping that should appear in the list.

Br,
Marvin

entry.title: Re: ABAP Cloud Developer Trial 2022 Available Now
entry.content:
Hello,

It always gets stuck as follows.

PS C:\Users\thomas> docker pull sapse/abap-cloud-developer-trial:ABAPTRIAL_2022
ABAPTRIAL_2022: Pulling from sapse/abap-cloud-developer-trial
93a70b6dd0a2: Downloading [========================> ] 7.43GB/15.4GB
fe0359f208b6: Already exists
fbff9d455e08: Already exists
2ca939ca1a03: Already exists
87b7d0095f25: Already exists
6f7c3878952c: Already exists

entry.title: Re: Accrual reversal for customer Rebate with different c...
entry.content:
Hi @mjcsant, 

In the source document, the accrual posting will always be on the bill-to used in the billing document (Customer 2/3/4). 
On the Settlement Document, the settle amount and reversal of accrual will always be tied to the Customer Contract owner (Single recipient) or to each relevant Customer (in Multiple Customer scenario). 

In your case, your case the easiest option would be: 

1. Don't post accruals on source document, but post accruals with Delta accrual. In that way, the accrual will be posting and reversing with Customer 1. 

2. Create contracts only for accrual posting on source document and reversal of accrual for Customer 2/3/4 - with Settlement type as "Only Accrual reversal" in Settlement data tab. 
Create another contract, only for settlement amount, no accrual, for Customer 1.

You have as well some functionalities - Transfer manager or Badi - to populate/redetermine fields at time of posting/reversing accrual, but this will not help you achieve what I believe you want to do. 

Regards, 
Gauthier

entry.title: Re: PL by Segment
entry.content:
Hi,

Try remove ' (single quote)

/* SELECT FROM [dbo].[JDT1] */
DECLARE FDT TimeStamp;
FDT := /* JDT1."DocDate" */[%0];

/* SELECT FROM [dbo].[JDT1] */
DECLARE TDT TimeStamp;
TDT := /* JDT1."DocDate" */[%1];

Hope this helps,

SonTran

entry.title: Re: ByDesign: Datasource for Production Confirmation incl...
entry.content:
Hello Henry. I appreciate your answer, however some points won´t work:

- We need to know scrap for what was made over the month and the start date doesn´t inform it because we have delays in production and some times we anticipate some orders. 

- we need also the reason at the same time because it´s important to know what´s scraped and why over the month.

i thought to use this screen/datasource i mentioned because i can get the transaction time(production time), Scrap quantity and Reason. it´s all we need.

Use Yield and Scrap datasource show all scraps however i cannot filter by date and join with Confirmation journal won´t work too because there have only confirmations with good products reported. if i report(as showed in screen) with zero quantity and 4 scrap it won´t be on Journal.

entry.title: SAP HANA Cloud release September 2024 Round-up
entry.content:
Dear SAP HANA Cloud Enthusiasts,

As we wrap up the third quarter of 2024, we're excited to share a complete collection of the latest videos, blog posts, and resources from the SAP HANA Cloud September 2024 release.

 

What’s New Teaser

Dive into SAP HANA Cloud's latest innovations with Lead Product Manager Thomas Hammer! His "What’s New" teaser offers an exciting and concise snapshot of his favorite features from the newest release.

Watch it now on YouTube.

What’s New blogpost

Intrigued by the teaser? Explore our "What’s New in SAP HANA Cloud in September 2024" blog post for an in-depth look and find valuable links for further insights.

Read it here!

What's New Webinar

Prefer watching over reading? Our 'What’s New' webinar is just for you! Join our Product experts for an in-depth exploration of the latest features. Watch anytime here!

 

Additional information


In addition to our regular release updates, make sure to check the following valuable content provided by our team:

  • Learn more about the latest features for Machine Learning in SAP HANA Cloud by @ChristophMorgen 
  • Find out more on the SAP HANA Cloud's Graphical View Modeling capabilities by @jan_zwickel 
  • Dive deep into Integrating SAP S/4HANA Data into SAP HANA Cloud Using SAP Datasphere by @Alexey Raskatov
  • Join our Early Adopter Care program:
    • SAP HANA Cloud for large instances
    • MDS Cubes in SAP HANA Cloud 
  • Join our Customer Engagement Initiative:
    • SAP HANA Database Migration to SAP HANA Cloud (XSC,XSA,CAP) 
Don’t miss out on all the content and remember to follow us in the SAP HANA Community.

Remember to check our content following the # whatsnewinsaphanacloud tag: here

Don’t forget to subscribe and follow SAP HANA Cloud on YouTube to always stay up-to-date regarding the most recent innovations in SAP HANA Cloud.
 
All the best,

Andrea on behalf of the SAP HANA Cloud team

#SAP HANA Cloud, SAP HANA database   #SAP HANA Cloud  

#whatsnewinsaphanacloud

entry.title: Globale Hierarchien, Bilanz und GuV strukturen
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

I have edited the balance sheet and P&L structures in the Global Hierarchy app and assigned unassigned accounts to specific positions. Editing in OB58 was not possible because the structure was integrated into the Global Hierarchy. I can see these changes in the Balance Sheet/P&L app, but they are not reflected in classic reports like S_ALR_87012284. Is it possible to update these as well?

ich habe die Bilanz- und GuV-Strukturen in der Global Hierarchy App bearbeitet und nicht zugeordnete Konten bestimmten Positionen zugeordnet. Die Bearbeitung im OB58 war nicht möglich, da die Struktur in die globale Hierarchie integriert war. Ich kann diese Änderungen in der Bilanz-/GuV-App sehen, aber sie spiegeln sich nicht in klassischen Berichten wie S_ALR_87012284 wider. Ist es möglich, diese auch zu aktualisieren?

BR

Natali

SAP HANA #S_alr_87012284

entry.title: Re: New End User File Upload in SAP Analytics Cloud QRC3 2024

entry.title: Consumption filter default
entry.content:
Hello,

I want to create a query for the querybrowser with a filter value in which the value is defaulted and calculated by $session.system_date - 30. So from today 30 days back. 

In my main view i did like this which works: 
dats_add_days(ZCDSV_ITEMCH_MAIN.current_Date_minus_30,-30, 'NULL') as current_Date_minus_30,

In my CUBE view i want to set this filterValue but i can only set static values and nothing which is dynamic like with $projection. 

So this works: 

current_Date_minus_30 as filterDate,

@AnalyticsDetails.query.axis: #ROWS

@Consumption.filter: { selectionType: #INTERVAL, mandatory: false }

@Consumption.filter.defaultValue: '20240902'

@EndUserText.label: 'UPD_date'

upd_date,


But this does not work:

current_Date_minus_30 as filterDate,

@AnalyticsDetails.query.axis: #ROWS

@Consumption.filter: { selectionType: #INTERVAL, mandatory: false }

@Consumption.filter.defaultValue: $projection.filterDate

@EndUserText.label: 'UPD_date'

upd_date,


can anyone help to set a predefined value which is calculated as a default filter value?

entry.title: Can I call add/switch user in multi user mode from my rule?
entry.content:
Hello experts,

this is regarding multi user mode in a branded mdk app.

Is there a prebuilt action/rule that i can use to call add/switch user from a screen/rule and not the prebuilt sap login screen?  I am trying to move these functionalities to a context menu/ screen button.

i can do this to logout but not add/switch user.

If there is no such prebuilt mechanism, how can i achieve this?

Thank you for your support,

RaulM

entry.title: Geração de remessa de cliente cria partida memo - F110
entry.content:
Olá, bom dia!

Estamos com um problema que as contas a receber ao gerar uma remessa de cliente na F110 o sistema está criando um memorando de partida com tp doc DY, mas  aparentemente quando o título é pago e liquidado um memorando de partida continua em aberto, gerando assim uma diferença. Alguém sabe me dizer se é normal criar partida memo ao gerar uma remessa? Se sim, como é liquidado? Usamos SAP S/4Hana 2022

entry.title: University of the People and SAP TechEd partner to empower the leaders of tomorrow
entry.content:
Brazilian educator and philosopher Paulo Freire once said, “Education does not change the world. Education changes people. People change the world.” However, with recent events like the COVID-19 pandemic, education access globally has been disrupted, with over 616 million students affected by school closures, risking an escalation in learning poverty where 50%, or approximately 825 million children worldwide, could lack basic literacy and numeracy skills by 2030. This crisis extends beyond immediate learning loss, impacting the future employability and innovative capabilities of today's youth.

At SAP, we know that empowering global learners to reach their highest potential by providing access to equitable education is the key to innovation and opportunity. We are proud to be part of the solution of the education and employment crisis, joining in the journey of so many learners as they look to use their education to make sustainable, positive impacts to change the world.

Since 2022, SAP has been a proud partner of  University of the People, the first nonprofit, tuition-free, American accredited, online university dedicated to global access to higher education. Established in 2009, the university provides virtual learning for more than 152,000 students worldwide, including over 18,000 refugees within its student population. University of the People serves students through the belief that higher education is a fundamental right for all. The virtual learning format of the university tackles the education crisis head on, providing education access for many students who don’t have a traditional brick-and-mortar University experience available due to various circumstances. Regardless of background, students are still able to complete their degrees, recognized by American accreditation standards. This sets up graduates for employment success and closes the education and employment gap for generations to come.

In celebration of SAP TechEd, we are excited to announce that SAP will be supporting 10 new scholarships for refugee women from around the world who are pursuing associate degrees in computer science at University of the People. These scholarships reinforce SAP’s commitment to University of the People and are a continuation of 10 scholarships awarded a year ago at SAP TechEd 2023. In addition to SAP TechEd, the company also broadly supports a full portfolio of scholarships in partnership with University of the People.

While these scholarships continue to build on our previous efforts, it's the personal stories of the students who benefit from this support that highlight the power of equitable education in action. One University of the People student, Kesanet Kidane from Eritrea, shares that she was inspired to think and dream big after seeing others around her unable to receive higher education. “I am a refugee in the US who was born and grew up in Eritrea. I wanted to see a world beyond the Eritrean border. I crossed the border at night hoping to see the other side of the world; the opportunist one. After waiting two years for my process in Ethiopia, I came to America four years ago for better opportunities in life and education.” After finishing high school, learning English, and living with her single mother and younger brother, Kesanet is eager to earn her degree in computer science while simultaneously working part-time. “After finishing at University of the People, I have a plan to get my master’s degree and get a job. Getting this scholarship is a path to fulfill my better education dream and a way to my better future.”

Another student, Maliha, was studying civil engineering at a university in Kabul, Afghanistan, when the Taliban took over the country. She shares her experience choosing University of the People to complete her education despite the challenges she faced during this difficult time. “I feared my dreams of earning a college degree had vanished forever. For the next six months, I hid in my home from the Taliban, not knowing what the future would bring.” After receiving a call from a former classmate informing her of the University of the People scholarships offered to Afghan women, Maliha immediately applied to finish her studies with a bachelor’s degree in computer science. “I was overjoyed that I could continue my education safely online from home. University of the People helped me to dream again about a better future and gave me the confidence to chase my dreams.” Recently, Maliha was accepted to a graduate program at Carnegie Mellon University and awarded a full scholarship. After traveling to Pakistan and receiving a visa to come to the United States, she was able to arrive safely and is excited to continue her studies at Carnegie Mellon.

Students like Kesanet and Maliha showcase how creating access to education for willing learners worldwide can be the first steppingstone to opportunity. Equipping students with the skills for tomorrow, especially while they are facing their own personal journeys of uprooting their lives for a better future, empowers confidence, inspires hope, and fuels innovation.

SAP is proud to support University of the People in celebration of SAP TechEd 2024, and to remain ongoing partners in the educational journeys for the changemakers of tomorrow. Learn more about SAP and University of the People’s partnership, along with our other education focused initiatives at SAP.

entry.title: Re: Troubleshooting SAP Certification discount code for special programs

entry.title: SAP BTP Build Work Zone (standard) - Fiori tiles keeps loading after expose from S/4HANA system.
entry.content:
Hi,

We use the BTP Service, SAP Build Work Zone (standard). We have set up catalogues, spaces and pages and roles on the S/4HANA system on premise. After these configurations we performed an expose via the SAP transaction /ui2/cdm3_exp_scope so that the apps are visible in build work zone.

What we see now is that some analytical tiles keeps loading. Does anyone have experience with this and does anyone have any tips on how to solve this?

Best regards,

Gene

M Harron Aziz

entry.title: Re: Can I call add/switch user in multi user mode from my...
entry.content: No, in order to switch users the current user needs to first logout by calling the logout action (typically with SkipReset set to true in the Logout action properties).  This will return the app to the passcode screen where the current user can either login again or another user can login using the Add/Switch User functionality.

entry.title: Re: SAP BTP Build Work Zone (standard) - Fiori tiles keeps loading after expose from S/4HANA system.
entry.content: Can you share the network trace? Some seems to be loaded so I guess it is for some apps only or is that a wrong interpretation?

entry.title: Re: 1st and 2nd Receipt days of supply calcluation
entry.content: Is there a reason why resulting number of days are same for both First receipt and second receipt days supply is same? Expected to see 2 different values.

entry.title: S/4HANA Cloud: requirements for deploying a collection to customer's tenant.
entry.content:
Hi,
I mean exactly certifications, interactions with SAP support and so on. Its' not about technical details. As I understand, this is the technical definition of the process of deploying collections to PROD tenant:
3-System Landscape and Transport Management 

For example, I (as a company) have DEV and TEST tenants. I've  created a collection of views, tested it on TEST tenant. Now I want to delver it to several PROD tenants of my customers. It's for free, because purpose of the collection is interaction between SAP cloud and our application that customer has.

What is official process for this? Should I have SAP some certificate as SAP developer ( SAP Certifications  )? When I deploy my collection, should I pass some official steps, I mean interaction with SAP?
I tried to find some how-to but I didn't.

entry.title: Re: 🟡 Techtoberfest 2024
entry.content: My current setup....

entry.title: Re: SAP error - Delivery already contained in shipment
entry.content:
Hello @Karthik34 

The error message says that the delivery is already planned either in a shipment (LE-TRA) or in a freight order (TM). The solution is to delete the shipment or the freight order and then delete the delivery.

The shortcut is to first delete the delivery item and save the delivery, then enter VL02N again and delete the delivery.

The first approach is the preferable one.

Best regards

Dominik Tylczynski

entry.title: IF YOU NEED A INSTAGRAM AND WHATSAPP HACKER HIRE ADWARE RECOVERY SPECIALIST
entry.content:
Infidelity investigations have become increasingly complex in the digital age, with the rise of online communication and the prevalence of hidden digital trails.  ADWARE RECOVERY SPECIALIST  is a specialized service that has emerged to address these challenges, offering sophisticated capabilities to uncover evidence of infidelity. At the heart of their approach is the ability to recover deleted or hidden data from a wide range of digital devices and online platforms. Through the use of advanced forensic techniques and proprietary software,  ADWARE RECOVERY SPECIALIST  can retrieve emails, text messages, chat logs, browsing histories, and other digital artifacts that may contain telltale signs of an affair. This includes recovering information from devices that have been wiped, encrypted, or intentionally obfuscated in an attempt to cover one's tracks. Beyond just data recovery,  ADWARE RECOVERY SPECIALIST also provides detailed analysis and reporting, piecing together the digital breadcrumbs to construct a comprehensive narrative of suspected infidelity. Their team of experts are adept at navigating the intricacies of online behavior, identifying patterns and anomalies that could indicate deception. With a deep understanding of how technology is misused in the context of infidelity, ADWARE RECOVERY SPECIALIST  is uniquely positioned to uncover the truth, giving clients the clarity and evidence they need to confront an unfaithful partner or move forward with confidence. 

Greetings.
